op_000005.source_statement: ∀ {Γ Δ Λ : List Tp} {A B C : Tp}, Γ ⇒ (A ⧹ B) → Δ ++ [B] ++ Λ ⇒ C → Δ ++ [A] ++ Γ ++ Λ ⇒ C
op_000005.formalized_statement: ∀ {Γ Δ Λ : List Tp} {A B C : Tp}, (Γ ⇒ (A ⧹ B)) → (Δ ++ [B] ++ Λ ⇒ C) → (Δ ++ [A] ++ Γ ++ Λ ⇒ C)
op_000005.statement_formalization_notes: Kept the proposition unchanged and added namespace/scoped opens so the repository notations `⇒` and `⧹` parse correctly.

op_000005.proof_sketch: Use `ldiv_invertible` to derive `[A] ++ Γ ⇒ B` from `Γ ⇒ A ⧹ B`, then apply `cut_admissible` against `Δ ++ [B] ++ Λ ⇒ C`; `simpa [List.append_assoc]` matches the target context.
op_000005.proof_text:
intro Γ Δ Λ A B C h₁ h₂
simpa [List.append_assoc] using cut_admissible (ldiv_invertible h₁) h₂
op_000005.lean_error_fingerprint: AutomatedTheoryConstruction/Scratch.loop.lean:9:82: error: unexpected token '⇒'; expected ')', ',' or ':' | toolchain=leanprover/lean4:v4.29.0-rc6

op_000005.proof_sketch: Bring the Lambek namespace and scoped notation into scope in `prelude_code`, then reuse `ldiv_invertible` to derive `[A] ++ Γ ⇒ B` from `Γ ⇒ A ⧹ B`. Apply `cut_admissible` with cut formula `B` against `Δ ++ [B] ++ Λ ⇒ C`, and finish by reassociating list append.
op_000005.proof_text:
intro Γ Δ Λ A B C h₁ h₂
simpa [List.append_assoc] using cut_admissible (ldiv_invertible h₁) h₂

op_000006.source_statement: ∀ {Γ Δ Λ : List Tp} {A B C : Tp}, Γ ⇒ (B ⧸ A) → Δ ++ [B] ++ Λ ⇒ C → Δ ++ Γ ++ [A] ++ Λ ⇒ C
op_000006.formalized_statement: ∀ {Γ Δ Λ : List Mathling.Lambek.ProductFree.Tp} {A B C : Mathling.Lambek.ProductFree.Tp}, Mathling.Lambek.ProductFree.Sequent Γ (Mathling.Lambek.ProductFree.Tp.rdiv B A) → Mathling.Lambek.ProductFree.Sequent (Δ ++ [B] ++ Λ) C → Mathling.Lambek.ProductFree.Sequent (Δ ++ Γ ++ [A] ++ Λ) C
op_000006.statement_formalization_notes: Repaired the parser issue by avoiding scoped notation and using fully qualified names for both `Sequent` and right division.

op_000006.proof_sketch: Invert the right-division hypothesis to get a derivation of `B` from `Γ ++ [A]`, then apply cut admissibility against the derivation of `C` from `Δ ++ [B] ++ Λ`, and simplify list associativity.
op_000006.proof_text:
intro hΓ hC
have hB : Mathling.Lambek.ProductFree.Sequent (Γ ++ [A]) B :=
  Mathling.Lambek.ProductFree.rdiv_invertible hΓ
simpa [List.append_assoc] using
  (Mathling.Lambek.ProductFree.cut_admissible hB hC)
op_000006.lean_error_fingerprint: AutomatedTheoryConstruction/Scratch.loop.002.lean:11:49: error(lean.unknownIdentifier): Unknown identifier `Γ` | AutomatedTheoryConstruction/Scratch.loop.002.lean:11:55: error(lean.unknownIdentifier): Unknown identifier `A` | AutomatedTheoryConstruction/Scratch.loop.002.lean:11:59: error(lean.unknownIdentifier): Unknown identifier `B`

op_000006.proof_sketch: The previous argument was correct, but the tactic script failed because `intro hΓ hC` consumed the leading implicit binders instead of the hypotheses. Introduce all implicit parameters explicitly first, derive `Γ ++ [A] ⇒ B` from `rdiv_invertible hΓ`, then apply `cut_admissible` to `hB` and `hC`, and finish by reassociating list append.
op_000006.proof_text:
intro Γ Δ Λ A B C hΓ hC
have hB : Mathling.Lambek.ProductFree.Sequent (Γ ++ [A]) B :=
  Mathling.Lambek.ProductFree.rdiv_invertible hΓ
simpa [List.append_assoc] using
  (Mathling.Lambek.ProductFree.cut_admissible hB hC)

op_000001.source_statement: ∀ {Γ Δ : List Tp} {A B : Tp}, Γ ⇒ (B ⧸ A) → Δ ⇒ A → Γ ++ Δ ⇒ B
op_000001.formalized_statement: ∀ {Γ Δ : List Tp} {A B : Tp}, Γ ⇒ (B ⧸ A) → Δ ⇒ A → Γ ++ Δ ⇒ B
op_000001.statement_formalization_notes: Added the namespace and scoped-notation prelude so `Tp`, `⧸`, and `⇒` resolve in a standalone statement.

op_000001.proof_sketch: Apply `rdiv_invertible` to turn `Γ ⇒ (B ⧸ A)` into `Γ ++ [A] ⇒ B`, then cut against `Δ ⇒ A` with `Λ := []`. The resulting sequent is `Γ ++ Δ ++ [] ⇒ B`, which simplifies to `Γ ++ Δ ⇒ B`.
op_000001.proof_text:
intro Γ Δ A B hΓ hΔ
have hBA : Γ ++ [A] ⇒ B := rdiv_invertible hΓ
have hcut : Γ ++ Δ ++ [] ⇒ B :=
  cut_admissible (Γ := Δ) (Δ := Γ) (Λ := []) hΔ (by simpa using hBA)
simpa using hcut
op_000001.lean_error_fingerprint: AutomatedTheoryConstruction/Scratch.loop.lean:9:76: error: unexpected token '⇒'; expected ':=', 'where' or '|' | AutomatedTheoryConstruction/Scratch.loop.lean:9:59: error(lean.unknownIdentifier): Unknown identifier `Tp` | Note: It is not possible to treat `Tp` as an implicitly bound variable here because the `autoImplicit` option is set to `false`.

op_000001.proof_sketch: The previous proof strategy is correct. The Lean failure came from missing namespace and scoped-notation setup for the fixed statement. Open `Mathling.Lambek.ProductFree`, use `rdiv_invertible` to get `Γ ++ [A] ⇒ B`, then apply `cut_admissible` with the cut formula proved by `Δ ⇒ A` and `Λ := []`, and simplify the trailing empty context.
op_000001.proof_text:
intro Γ Δ A B hΓ hΔ
have hBA : Γ ++ [A] ⇒ B := rdiv_invertible hΓ
have hcut : Γ ++ Δ ++ [] ⇒ B :=
  cut_admissible (Γ := Δ) (Δ := Γ) (Λ := []) hΔ (by simpa using hBA)
simpa using hcut

op_000002.source_statement: ∀ {Γ Δ : List Tp} {A B : Tp}, Γ ⇒ (A ⧹ B) → Δ ⇒ A → Δ ++ Γ ⇒ B
op_000002.formalized_statement: ∀ {Γ Δ : List Mathling.Lambek.ProductFree.Tp} {A B : Mathling.Lambek.ProductFree.Tp}, Mathling.Lambek.ProductFree.Sequent Γ (Mathling.Lambek.ProductFree.Tp.ldiv A B) → Mathling.Lambek.ProductFree.Sequent Δ A → Mathling.Lambek.ProductFree.Sequent (Δ ++ Γ) B
op_000002.statement_formalization_notes: Repaired the statement by fully qualifying `Tp`, `Sequent`, and `ldiv` so it parses without relying on local opens or scoped notation.

op_000002.proof_sketch: Use the verified theorem `ldiv_invertible` to turn `Γ ⇒ A ⧹ B` into `[A] ++ Γ ⇒ B`, then apply `cut_admissible` with `Δ ⇒ A` and simplify the empty-prefix concatenation.
op_000002.proof_text:
intro Γ Δ A B hΓ hΔ
simpa using
  Mathling.Lambek.ProductFree.cut_admissible hΔ
    (Mathling.Lambek.ProductFree.ldiv_invertible hΓ)
op_000002.lean_error_excerpt: AutomatedTheoryConstruction/Scratch.loop.002.lean:13:6: error: Application type mismatch: The argument
op_000002.lean_error_fingerprint: AutomatedTheoryConstruction/Scratch.loop.002.lean:13:6: error: Application type mismatch: The argument | Mathling.Lambek.ProductFree.ldiv_invertible hΓ | has type

op_000002.proof_sketch: Use `ldiv_invertible` to turn `hΓ : Γ ⇒ A ⧹ B` into `[A] ++ Γ ⇒ B`. Then apply `cut_admissible` to `hΔ : Δ ⇒ A` with explicit context parameters `Γ := Δ`, `Δ := []`, `Λ := Γ`, so the right premise matches `[] ++ [A] ++ Γ ⇒ B`. Finally simplify `[] ++ Δ ++ Γ`.
op_000002.proof_text:
intro Γ Δ A B hΓ hΔ
have hcut : Mathling.Lambek.ProductFree.Sequent ([] ++ Δ ++ Γ) B :=
  Mathling.Lambek.ProductFree.cut_admissible (Γ := Δ) (Δ := []) (Λ := Γ) hΔ
    (by simpa using Mathling.Lambek.ProductFree.ldiv_invertible hΓ)
simpa using hcut

op_000007.source_statement: ∀ {Γ : List Tp} {A : Tp}, (∀ x ∈ Γ, is_atom x) → (Γ ⇒ A ↔ ∃ s : String, Γ = [#s] ∧ A = #s)
op_000007.formalized_statement: ∀ {Γ : List Mathling.Lambek.ProductFree.Tp} {A : Mathling.Lambek.ProductFree.Tp}, (∀ x ∈ Γ, Mathling.Lambek.ProductFree.is_atom x) → (Mathling.Lambek.ProductFree.Sequent Γ A ↔ ∃ s : String, Γ = [Mathling.Lambek.ProductFree.Tp.atom s] ∧ A = Mathling.Lambek.ProductFree.Tp.atom s)
op_000007.statement_formalization_notes: Repaired minimally by replacing notation (`⇒`, `#`) with fully qualified names to avoid parser/scope issues while preserving the original meaning.

op_000007.proof_sketch: Assume the claimed universal equivalence. Specialize it to Γ = [Tp.atom "a"] and A = Tp.ldiv (Tp.rdiv (Tp.atom "b") (Tp.atom "a")) (Tp.atom "b"). The context hypothesis holds because the only element of Γ is atomic. Build a derivation of Sequent Γ A by combining Sequent.ax for `a` and `b`, then `Sequent.rdiv_l` to obtain `[b / a, a] ⇒ b`, and finally `Sequent.ldiv_r` to obtain `[a] ⇒ (b / a) \ b`. The specialized equivalence then yields an `s : String` such that the succedent equals `Tp.atom s`, contradicting the fact that it is an `ldiv` term.
op_000007.proof_text:
intro h
have h_atoms :
    ∀ x ∈ ([Mathling.Lambek.ProductFree.Tp.atom "a"] :
      List Mathling.Lambek.ProductFree.Tp),
      Mathling.Lambek.ProductFree.is_atom x := by
  intro x hx
  simp at hx
  rcases hx with rfl
  simp [Mathling.Lambek.ProductFree.is_atom]
have ha :
    Mathling.Lambek.ProductFree.Sequent
      [Mathling.Lambek.ProductFree.Tp.atom "a"]
      (Mathling.Lambek.ProductFree.Tp.atom "a") :=
  Mathling.Lambek.ProductFree.Sequent.ax
have hb :
    Mathling.Lambek.ProductFree.Sequent
      [Mathling.Lambek.ProductFree.Tp.atom "b"]
      (Mathling.Lambek.ProductFree.Tp.atom "b") :=
  Mathling.Lambek.ProductFree.Sequent.ax
have h_rdiv :
    Mathling.Lambek.ProductFree.Sequent
      [ Mathling.Lambek.ProductFree.Tp.rdiv
          (Mathling.Lambek.ProductFree.Tp.atom "b")
          (Mathling.Lambek.ProductFree.Tp.atom "a"),
        Mathling.Lambek.ProductFree.Tp.atom "a" ]
      (Mathling.Lambek.ProductFree.Tp.atom "b") := by
  simpa using
    (Mathling.Lambek.ProductFree.Sequent.rdiv_l
      (Γ := [])
      (Δ := [Mathling.Lambek.ProductFree.Tp.atom "a"])
      (Λ := [])
      (A := Mathling.Lambek.ProductFree.Tp.atom "a")
      (B := Mathling.Lambek.ProductFree.Tp.atom "b")
      (C := Mathling.Lambek.ProductFree.Tp.atom "b")
      ha
      hb)
have h_seq :
    Mathling.Lambek.ProductFree.Sequent
      [Mathling.Lambek.ProductFree.Tp.atom "a"]
      (Mathling.Lambek.ProductFree.Tp.ldiv
        (Mathling.Lambek.ProductFree.Tp.rdiv
          (Mathling.Lambek.ProductFree.Tp.atom "b")
          (Mathling.Lambek.ProductFree.Tp.atom "a"))
        (Mathling.Lambek.ProductFree.Tp.atom "b")) := by
  refine Mathling.Lambek.ProductFree.Sequent.ldiv_r
    (Γ := [Mathling.Lambek.ProductFree.Tp.atom "a"])
    (A := Mathling.Lambek.ProductFree.Tp.rdiv
      (Mathling.Lambek.ProductFree.Tp.atom "b")
      (Mathling.Lambek.ProductFree.Tp.atom "a"))
    (B := Mathling.Lambek.ProductFree.Tp.atom "b")
    ?_
    ?_
  · simp
  · simpa using h_rdiv
have hs :
    ∃ s : String,
      ([Mathling.Lambek.ProductFree.Tp.atom "a"] :
        List Mathling.Lambek.ProductFree.Tp) =
        [Mathling.Lambek.ProductFree.Tp.atom s] ∧
      Mathling.Lambek.ProductFree.Tp.ldiv
          (Mathling.Lambek.ProductFree.Tp.rdiv
            (Mathling.Lambek.ProductFree.Tp.atom "b")
            (Mathling.Lambek.ProductFree.Tp.atom "a"))
          (Mathling.Lambek.ProductFree.Tp.atom "b") =
        Mathling.Lambek.ProductFree.Tp.atom s :=
  (h
    (Γ := [Mathling.Lambek.ProductFree.Tp.atom "a"])
    (A := Mathling.Lambek.ProductFree.Tp.ldiv
      (Mathling.Lambek.ProductFree.Tp.rdiv
        (Mathling.Lambek.ProductFree.Tp.atom "b")
        (Mathling.Lambek.ProductFree.Tp.atom "a"))
      (Mathling.Lambek.ProductFree.Tp.atom "b"))
    h_atoms).mp h_seq
rcases hs with ⟨s, _, hA⟩
cases hA
op_000007.counterexample_text: Take Γ = [Tp.atom "a"] and A = Tp.ldiv (Tp.rdiv (Tp.atom "b") (Tp.atom "a")) (Tp.atom "b"). Every element of Γ is atomic. There is a derivation of `Sequent Γ A`: `Sequent.ax` gives `[a] ⇒ a` and `[b] ⇒ b`, `Sequent.rdiv_l` yields `[b / a, a] ⇒ b`, and `Sequent.ldiv_r` yields `[a] ⇒ (b / a) \ b`. But `A` is an `ldiv` term, so it cannot equal `Tp.atom s` for any string `s`. Hence the claimed equivalence fails for this Γ and A.
